content: The alternative scoring methodology provides a simplified, percentage-based compliance assessment suitable for cross-institution comparison. The total score is calculated by summing points across several dimensions: PCAF Status (max 20%): Signatory = 20%, Mentioned only = 10%, Not mentioned = 0%. This rewards formal commitment to the PCAF standard. Part A — Financed Emissions (max 40%): Reported with quantified values = 40%, Partial disclosure = 20%, Missing = 0%. This is the largest single component, reflecting the centrality of financed emissions. Part B — Facilitated Emissions (max 15%, banks/bancassurance only): Reported = 15%, Partial = 7.5%, Missing = 0%, N/A = 0%. For non-bank entities, this component is excluded and the total is renormalized. Part C — Insurance-Associated Emissions (max 15%, insurers/reinsurers only): Reported = 15%, Partial = 7.5%, Missing = 0%, N/A = 0%. For non-insurance entities, this component is excluded and the total is renormalized. Asset Class Detail (max 25%): Each of the 10 PCAF asset classes scores 2.5% if reported or 1.25% if partial. This rewards breadth of coverage. The total score maps to maturity levels: 0-19% = Not Started/Initial, 20-39% = Initial/Developing, 40-59% = Developing/Defined, 60-79% = Defined/Advanced, 80-100% = Advanced/Leading.
